name: Chicken with Orange Peel, Szechuan Style
recipeIngredient: 2 large Whole chicken breasts, skinned and boned
1 teaspoon Minced, peeled ginger root, OR
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a vegetable peeler, cut the peel from the orange into 1 1/2-inch-wide pieces, taking care not to cut into the white membrane. Cut the pieces into 1 1/2-inch-long strips.
2. Place the peeled orange strips on a small cookie sheet and let them dry slightly in an oven preheated to 200°F for about 30 minutes.
3. Cut the chicken into 1 1/2-inch pieces. In a medium bowl, mix well with the chicken, soy sauce, sherry, green onions, crushed red pepper, and ginger (fresh or ground).
4. In a small bowl, mix together cornstarch, sugar, salt, and orange juice. Cover both bowls and refrigerate until ready to use.
5. About 15 minutes before serving, heat a 10-inch skillet over medium heat with hot oil. Using a slotted spoon, stir-fry the orange peel strips for about 2 minutes or until they are crisp and lightly browned. Drain on paper towels.
6. In the remaining oil in the skillet, heat over high heat. Stir-fry the chicken mixture for about 4 minutes, or until the chicken loses its pink color and becomes tender. Stir in the orange juice mixture, then add it to the chicken and stir-fry until slightly thickened and coating the chicken.
7. Spoon the dish onto a warm platter and sprinkle with the dried orange peel pieces. Makes 4 servings.
Enjoy!
